explanations.17: This variable stores a flag (--env-file) plus a filename together, so when you use ${ENV} later, it expands to the complete flag and file path as one piece.
explanations.20: This sets an environment variable for docker compose itself (not a make variable), so the syntax is different—it's COMPOSE_FILE= with no space before the equals sign.
explanations.23: This creates a special format string for printing colored text in the terminal; the escape codes tell your terminal to make text bold cyan, then reset the color back to normal.
explanations.27: This converts the container name to hexadecimal using a shell command—vscode needs this special format to identify which Docker container to connect to.
explanations.50: This uses awk (a text-processing tool) to parse the Makefile itself and extract all the comments with ## to auto-generate a help menu showing available commands.
